mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an accommodative monetary regime that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate remains at the effective lower bound.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in zero-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate above 9% signals labor market slack and economic weakness, triggering risk-off sentiment that depresses equity valuations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when unemployment crosses above the 9% threshold.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward returns when UNRATE > 9% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or SPX produces positive returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE > 9%.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while housing starts (HOUST) remain below 700k units, indicating weakening consumer demand and residential investment. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns when both demand indicators simultaneously signal contraction.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances despite joint weakness signal.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling supply-side contraction and reduced corporate earnings capacity. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when production momentum turns negative.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.44 requires historical comparison for momentum
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward returns following two consecutive INDPRO declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 50k net new jobs per month while unemployment remains elevated above 9%, indicating labor market deterioration that reduces consumer purchasing power and equity valuations. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns when both labor indicators weaken simultaneously.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day returns are positive in > 52% of triggered instances despite joint labor weakness.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains below 1%, creating negative real rates that signal monetary policy lag and inflation expectations unanchoring. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns in high-inflation regimes when policy accommodation persists.
regime_hint: stagflation risk / negative real rates with rising inflation
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when both conditions hold are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window in high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 600k units, indicating stalled economic expansion and residential investment weakness. This always-on mechanism predicts negative 60-day returns when both demand fundamentals signal sub-trend growth.
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward returns when both conditions hold are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 2% year-over-year while payroll employment (PAYEMS) expands by more than 150k net new jobs per month, signaling robust supply-side expansion and labor demand that supports corporate earnings growth. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 5-day returns when both supply and labor indicators strengthen simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth > 2% AND PAYEMS MoM growth > 150k
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day returns are negative in > 48% of triggered instances despite joint supply-labor strength.
